ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-04-2026 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-09-2026 Continued To May-04-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Christopher Moore, Esq., for The Demiris Law Firm.
No other appearances.

The court notes the absence of the Administrator and subsequently, discusses the status of this matter with counsel.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Administrator in pro per Heesho Bushnell for May-04-2026 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 204 for failure to Appear and to show cause why the court should not Suspend or Remove her as Administrator. Status on Close of Estate is continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Michael McNaughton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-17-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-16-2025 Continued To Feb-09-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Heesoo Bushnell, Administrator in Pro Per (via Zoom).
Christopher M. Moore for The Demiris Law Firm, P.C. (via Zoom).

The Court reviewed the recent filing by Ms. Bushnell concerning an unclaimed property issue involving Transamerica. Ms. Bushnell confirmed that the Transamerica issue is the final matter remaining before a petition for final distribution can be filed.

Ms. Bushnell informed the Court that the unclaimed property department estimated up to 180 days to resolve the matter and requested an additional four weeks for follow-up and preparation of final documents. Mr. Moore had no objection to the timeline.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to February 9, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Approval Of Ordinary And Extraordinary Compensation, And Reimbursement For Costs Advanced
Off Calendar Mar-19-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Moore for Demiris Law Firm, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Heesoo Bushnell, Administrator.

Ms. Bushnell stated she only received an invoice for $4,175 and was unaware of additional fees. Mr. Moore confirmed that all fee records were provided with the petition.

The court inquired as to when the administrator would file a petition for final distribution. Ms. Bushnell stated she intended to file by October due to issues with unclaimed property from Transamerica. The court then inquired of Mr. Moore as to whether he planned to file a 12202 petition to compel the administrator to petition for final distribution. Mr. Moore stated he had not considered filing a 12202 petition but noted uncertainty about outstanding tasks.

After discussion, the court took the Petition for Approval of Ordinary and Extraordinary Compensation under submission for further review. The court set a status hearing on close of estate for June 16, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-10-2022 Continued To Apr-18-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Moore, via ZOOM, for Heesoo Bushnell, Administrator.

The court asks for an update regarding bond. Mr. Moore reports that a beneficiary of the estate has passed-away and that a petition in Contra Costa county is set for hearing on Feb-17-2022. He anticipates his client will be appointed and will obtain a bond waiver. The court reminds Mr. Moore that the pursuant to inventory and appraisals filed, the existing bond appears to be insufficient. The court then confirms with Mr. Moore that one of two creditor's claims has been paid. The second claim was rejected. Notice of rejection was given and there was no challenge to the rejection filed within the time limits allowed.

After additional discussion, the Status on Close of Estate is continued to Apr-18-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody (via ZOOM). Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jun-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Moore, via ZOOM, counsel for Heesoo Bushnell, Administrator.

The court begins by confirming with counsel that a partial Inventory and Appraisal has been filed and how much time he will need to have this estate ready for final distribution. Counsel reports that the main issue now is that the Administrator has changed attorneys in an eviction proceeding and the parties are currently in settlement discussions and the property cannot be sold until that issue is resolved. After additional discussion, the court proposes a hearing date for status on close of estate on January 10, 2022. Counsel is advised to file either a Petition for Final Distribution or Petition on Status of Administration no later than two weeks prior, in order for that hearing date to be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Mar-17-2021 Continued To Jun-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Moore appeared by zoom for Heesoo Bushnell (Petitioner/Administrator).

The Court stated that there are a few issues that were identified by the Probate Examiner and questions why there is no inventory and appraisal ("I&A"). Mr. Moore stated that they discovered an account, that they are looking to confirm ownership, that there is the issue of the real property regarding litigation of eviction of the squatters, and that he was informed by the eviction Attorney that they were looking to see if there would be a change in the eviction moritorium in February, but they did not see a change. Mr. Moore also stated that he does not think the property can be appraised until all of the parties are out of the property, that a partial I&A regarding the investments is currently with the Referee and is being processed, and that he does not have enough information to advise the Court as to when the property will be able to be appraised. In addition, Mr. Moore provided the case number (MSP 20 01515) for the conservatorship case in Contra Costa County, stated that he has no problem with providing interval status reports and that he has informed the Administrator that they need to get the eviction case moving towards trial. The Report on Status of Administration is continued to June 9, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Moore is to provide an update by May 26, 2021 by way of a declaration.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-18-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Moore appeared by zoom for Heesoo Bushnell.

The Court noted that no one else was present. Mr. Moore stated that he spoke with Ms. Hinshaw by e-mail, that he was told she would not be appearing here as she had another hearing, and that she indicated that she did not plan to object, but she wanted to make sure that her Client can assert claims to the personal items. There being no objections, the Petition for Determination of Entitlement to Estate Distribution is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
